sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
One of the most dramatic first corners in F1. Approach uphill with heavy braking around the 100m board, shifting down to 1st or 2nd gear. The apex is almost blind due to the crest, so aim early and cut in late. Stay tight on the inside kerb, and power out smoothly — watch for rear traction loss as the track flattens out.

Inspired by Silverstones Maggotts and Becketts corner complex, this is a fast and technical section. Take Turn 2 flat, then lift slightly for Turn 3. Flick through Turns 4–6 in 6th gear, with minimal braking. You must maintain momentum and utilise the entire available track width. Line choice is everything — misplace one apex and you will find yourself off-line for the rest of the section.

Turn 7 is a fast left kink, immediately followed by a snappy change of direction into Turns 8 and 9. Take them in 5th or 4th gear, depending on setup. Keep it tight and be smooth on the wheel — balance and rhythm matter more than aggression here.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
A flat-out left bend that sets you up for Turn 11. Position the car well and avoid running too wide. A prime overtaking spot. Brake at the 100m board, down to 1st or 2nd gear. This corner tightens late, so trail braking helps the car rotate. Watch out for traction loss on exit — it leads into the longest straight of the circuit.

Engage your DRS and deploy ERS here during qualifying or overtaking. This straight is long enough for slipstreaming to be highly effective. Line up your pass or prepare to defend heading into Turn 12.

Another major overtaking zone. Brake at around the 100m marker, drop to 2nd gear, and stay tight. Avoid overshooting, as the wide run-off tempts you into braking too late. Rotate early for a clean exit into the stadium sector.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
This is a slow-speed left-right combo. Brake gently, keep the car stable in 2nd or 3rd gear, and avoid overusing the kerbs. Applying smooth throttle input is crucial to maintaining momentum through Turn 15.

A wide double-apex left-hander. You can experiment with different lines here — some take a late apex to straighten the exit; others hug the inside. Use 2nd or 3rd gear, and be patient with the throttle to avoid spinning your rear wheels.

Inspired by Istanbuls Turn 8, this triple-apex right is taken in 5th or 6th gear with a lift on entry. Its crucial to stay steady on the throttle and find the rhythm. If you get the balance right, you can maintain serious speed through here — a rewarding section when done well.

A medium-speed left-hander. Brake lightly and clip the inside curb in 4th gear. Beware of track limits — its easy to run wide and invalidate your lap.

Brake at around 75m, down to 2nd gear. Hug the inside kerb and focus on getting the best possible exit — this leads onto the start/finish straight. Open DRS early and deploy any leftover ERS for a strong run into Turn 1.
